description_long: This object is a detailed model of a crustacean, resembling a mantis shrimp or similar marine species. Its elongated body is segmented, with a robust cephalothorax and a flexible abdomen, both covered in a mottled brown and beige exoskeleton. The front features two prominent claws (chelae) and long, slender antennae extending forward, while multiple walking legs are visible along the underside. The eyes are set on short stalks at the front of the head, providing a wide field of vision. The surface appears hard and slightly rough, typical of a crustacean's chitinous shell, with subtle color gradations and naturalistic texturing. This object would be found as food or display in urban markets, restaurants, or educational exhibits.
description: A realistic, segmented crustacean with a mottled brown exoskeleton, prominent claws, and long antennae. The body is elongated with multiple legs and eyes on stalks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crustacean, highlighting the segmented body, claws, and extended antennae.
description_view_1: Front view emphasizing the face, eyes on stalks, and the symmetry of the claws and antennae.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other profile of the body and legs.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail fan and the arrangement of the walking legs along the abdomen.
